codex.apica.alchemy.pipe_t2.title: Improved Pipes
codex.apica.alchemy.pipe_t3.title: Royal Pipes
codex.apica.alchemy.pipe_t4.title: Nectar Pipes
codex.apica.alchemy.alembic.title: Alembic
codex.apica.alchemy.incubator.title: Incubator
codex.apica.alchemy.honey_furnace.title: Honey Furnace
codex.apica.alchemy.royal_furnace.title: Royal Furnace
codex.apica.alchemy.nectar_furnace.title: Nectar Furnace
codex.apica.alchemy.honey_furnace.lore.p: Wondered if honey could replace coal. Rigged a furnace to draw from a tank instead. It works. Burns clean, no ash, and smelts at §6§ltwice the speed§r.
The energy in the honey converts to heat more efficiently than anything I have tried before.
codex.apica.alchemy.royal_furnace.lore.p: Royal jelly is far more potent than plain honey. Adapted the design to handle it. The heat output was so high it could run §6§ltwo slots in parallel§r at §6§ltriple speed§r.
Had to reinforce the walls twice before they stopped cracking.
Pure nectar. Almost did not dare use it as fuel. The first test melted the lining. Rebuilt the whole thing with sturdier materials. §6§lTwo slots§r, §6§lsix times speed§r.
The ore barely has time to glow before it is done. I keep my distance when it is running.
codex.apica.logistics.liquid_pipe.lore.p: Hauling fluids by bucket is tedious and imprecise. The Liquid Pipe solves this. Honey, water, anything §6§lliquid moves§r between tanks and machines without spilling.
Same control scheme as item pipes. Activating a §6§ljunction§r between §6§ltwo pipes§r §6§ltoggles§r the link. Activating a §6§ljunction§r to a §6§lcompatible block§r starts §6§lextraction§r.
Applying §6§ldye§r to distinguish fluid lines or clean them with any §6§lhoney comb§r help me keep things readable when the setup grows.
If a pipe gets stuck or I need to flush the line, §6§lShift + Right Click§r with an §6§lempty hand§r instantly drains the internal buffer.
codex.apica.alchemy.centrifuge_t1.lore.p: The manual crank limits throughput. But I have observed something: the honey contains enough latent energy to sustain a mechanical process if channeled correctly. A §6§lPowered Centrifuge§r that consumes honey as fuel.
It works. The machine feeds on the very substance it processes. Output is consistent, hands-free. This changes the scale of what I can accomplish.
codex.apica.alchemy.centrifuge_t2.lore.p: Tried running royal-grade combs through the standard centrifuge. The drum seized, bearings overheated. The jelly is too viscous for those internals.
Rebuilt the whole assembly. Reinforced drum, faster rotation, larger tank. §6§lNoticeably faster§r too. Whatever is stored in these materials, there is a lot of it.
codex.apica.alchemy.centrifuge_t3.lore.p: The powered centrifuge, even upgraded, cannot keep pace with the volume of material I now process. Nectar-grade combs demand something fundamentally different. A dedicated multiblock structure.

The Powered Centrifuge III. A three-by-three formation with reservoir feeds and dedicated output channels. Processing time drops to a third of the base model. At this scale, the operation runs itself.
codex.apica.alchemy.portable_tank.lore.p: Left honey in a bucket overnight. By morning it had gone dull, like the energy just bled out into the air. Needed something sealed. Built a small Portable Tank. §6§lEight thousand millibuckets§r. Not much, but the honey inside stays stable.
codex.apica.alchemy.tank.lore.p1: The small tank ran dry faster than I could refill it. Honey, royal jelly, nectar. Too many fluids, not enough room.
codex.apica.alchemy.tank.lore.p2: Built interlocking §6§lFluid Tanks§r. Stack them together and they merge into a single reservoir. Each block added increases the total capacity. §6§lNo upper limit§r that I have found so far.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t1.lore.p1: Submerging materials in concentrated honey transforms them at a molecular level. Iron emerges as §6§lHoneyed Iron§r, structurally reinforced and faintly warm. Glass becomes translucent amber.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t1.lore.p2: The §6§lInfuser§r automates what I first did by hand. Two hundred ticks per transformation. Every batch confirms what I suspected: the honey is not just food. It is a medium for something far more potent.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t2.lore.p1: Honey infusion was only the beginning. Royal jelly, with its higher concentration of energy, produces dramatically different results. Gold exposed to royal jelly takes on a crystalline structure I have never documented.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t2.lore.p2: §6§lRoyal Gold§r. The material retains the malleability of gold but gains a resonance, almost as if it vibrates at a frequency too low to hear. I am starting to understand the scale of what this power can do.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t3.lore.p1: Nectar infusion requires containment beyond what a single machine provides. The energy released during transmutation would crack any standard vessel. A multiblock structure distributes the stress.
codex.apica.alchemy.infuser_t3.lore.p2: The §6§lInfuser Array§r. Diamond submerged in concentrated nectar emerges as §6§lNectar Diamond§r. The carbon lattice is fundamentally rewritten. This is no longer chemistry. I do not have a name for what this is.
codex.apica.alchemy.alembic.lore.p1: The honey is not the source of power. It is a carrier. The bees collect something invisible and deposit it in the honey. Royal jelly concentrates it further, but neither is the pure form.
codex.apica.alchemy.alembic.lore.p2: The §6§lAlembic§r distills honey and royal jelly together, producing §6§lNectar§r. Clear, radiant, warm to the touch. §6§lThe real work begins here.§r
codex.apica.alchemy.incubator.lore.p1: Bee larvae cannot mature under arbitrary conditions. Temperature, humidity, and time must be precisely controlled. Some specimens are far more demanding than others.
codex.apica.alchemy.incubator.lore.p2: The Incubator provides the exact thermal profile each larva requires. The stronger the bee, the longer it seems to take to hatch.
codex.apica.logistics.crystallyzer.title: Crystallizer
codex.apica.logistics.liquid_pipe.title: Liquid Pipe
codex.apica.logistics.item_pipe.title: Item Pipe
codex.apica.logistics.item_filter.title: Item Filter
codex.apica.logistics.item_filter.desc: A filter that controls which items can pass through a pipe.
codex.apica.logistics.item_filter.lore.p: The endless chaos of items flowing through our pipe networks has led me to devise a solution to filter items passing by.

§6§lTwo modes§r are available: §6§lAccept§r, only allowing listed items through, and §6§lDeny§r, blocking them. A priority system has also been added. Use §6§lShift + Right Click§r §6§lto remove§r a filter from its pipe.
codex.apica.logistics.relay.title: Storage Relay
codex.apica.logistics.interface.title: Storage Terminal
codex.apica.logistics.import.title: Import Interface
codex.apica.logistics.export.title: Export Interface
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t2.title: Improved Pipes
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t3.title: Royal Pipes
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t4.title: Nectar Pipes
codex.apica.logistics.item_pipe.lore.p1: Too much time spent carrying solid items between machines by hand. Built an Item Pipe. Hollow channel with an internal conveyor. Connect two inventories and the §6§litems move§r on their own.
codex.apica.logistics.item_pipe.lore.p2: Made useful interactions. Activating a §6§ljunction§r between §6§ltwo pipes§r §6§ltoggles§r the link. No need to break anything. Activating a §6§ljunction§r to a §6§lcompatible block§r starts §6§lextraction§r from that block.
codex.apica.logistics.item_pipe.lore.p3: The network gets tangled fast. Applying §6§ldye§r to a pipe colors the route for easier tracking. Any §6§lhoney comb§r cleans it back to the original look.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_controller: The operation has outgrown what pipes alone can manage. Dozens of containers, hundreds of item types. I needed a system that could organize everything.

The §6§lStorage Controller§r directs delivery bees to sort, store, and retrieve items across a network of registered containers. §6§lShift+right-click§r to enter §6§ledit mode§r to add elements to the network. §6§lIt consumes honey§r to fuel the bees' flights.
codex.apica.logistics.relay.lore.p1: The controller has a limited reach. Distant containers fall outside its detection range. I needed waypoints to bridge the gaps.
codex.apica.logistics.relay.lore.p2: The Storage Relay §6§lextends the network§r. Each relay registers local containers and connects to other nodes, forming a traversable path for delivery bees. The network can grow as large as needed.
codex.apica.logistics.interface.lore.p1: Spent twenty minutes looking for a single iron ingot. Opened every barrel, every chest. It was in the third one I checked the first time but I missed it. This does not scale.
codex.apica.logistics.interface.lore.p2: Built a terminal that shows everything in the network at once. Search, deposit, request. The bees fetch whatever I need. I just sit at the §6§lStorage Terminal§r and point.
codex.apica.logistics.import.lore.p1: Got tired of hand-feeding machines. The centrifuge runs dry, the infuser sits idle, all because I forgot to restock a slot.
codex.apica.logistics.import.lore.p2: The Import Interface pulls items from the network into an adjacent inventory. Set the §6§lfilters§r, set the §6§lquantity§r, and the bees keep it supplied. One less thing to babysit.
codex.apica.logistics.export.lore.p1: Output slots fill up. Machines stop. I come back to find everything jammed because I did not empty a chest in time.
codex.apica.logistics.export.lore.p2: The Export Interface watches adjacent inventories and sends their contents into the network. Configurable §6§lthresholds§r keep a minimum stock in place. The production line runs whether I am watching or not.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t2.lore.p1: A minor throughput improvement for growing operations. Slightly larger buffer and faster transfer. Not a breakthrough, but a practical upgrade.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t2.lore.p2: Improved Pipes. Increased buffer, increased transfer rate. Enough to keep a mid-scale operation from bottlenecking.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t3.lore.p1: Standard pipes show strain under sustained royal jelly transport. The energy in royal-grade fluids erodes standard linings over time. Royal materials solve this.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t3.lore.p2: Royal Pipes. Internal channels lined with crystallized royal jelly. Throughput increases dramatically and the material resists degradation. Essential for any operation handling royal-grade fluids.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t4.lore.p1: The final refinement. Nectar-infused channel walls that resonate with the fluid rather than resist it. Transport becomes nearly frictionless.
codex.apica.logistics.pipe_t4.lore.p2: Nectar Pipes. The throughput ceiling I assumed was physical turns out to have been a material limitation. These channels handle volumes I could not have imagined at the start of this work.

item.apica.essence.role.diurnal: Reduces honey cost during the day
item.apica.essence.role.nocturnal: Reduces honey cost during the night
item.apica.essence.role.insomnia: Reduces honey cost at all times
codex.apica.bees.hive.title: Hive
codex.apica.bees.hive.desc: The foundation of bee keeping. Place a Hive to start attracting and housing bees.
codex.apica.bees.hive.lore: Every journey begins with a hive. This enchanted structure draws in wild bees with its resonant warmth.
codex.apica.bees.incubator.title: Incubator
codex.apica.bees.incubator.desc: Breeds new bee species by combining two parent bees with specific conditions.
codex.apica.bees.incubator.lore: The incubator recreates ideal conditions for crossbreeding. Two compatible species, the right environment, and patience.
codex.apica.bees.pollen_pot.title: Pollen Pot
codex.apica.bees.pollen_pot.desc: Stores a single type of pollen.
codex.apica.bees.pollen_pot.lore.p1: Pollen kept scattering across the workbench. Different types mixing together, ruining samples. Shaped a small clay pot to hold §6§lone pollen type§r at a time, up to sixteen doses.
codex.apica.bees.pollen_pot.lore.p2: Right-click to deposit, punch to retrieve. Keeps things organized. The incubator reads from it directly, which saves a step.
codex.apica.bees.resonator.title: Resonator
codex.apica.bees.resonator.lore.p1: Cannot tell the species apart by sight alone. Built a device that emits §6§lwave pulses§r and reads how the bee reacts. Each species responds at different frequencies.
codex.apica.bees.resonator.lore.p2: Took some calibration, but once the parameters are dialed in the analysis runs on its own. Maps out §6§ltraits§r, §6§lspecies data§r. Results get logged here automatically. Should have built this sooner.
codex.apica.bees.essence_injector.title: Essence Injector
codex.apica.bees.essence_injector.desc: Applies extracted essences to modify bee traits.
codex.apica.bees.essence_injector.lore.p: Had a thought. If essences carry a bee's traits, maybe I can feed them back into a different specimen. Put a §6§lbee§r and an §6§lessence§r in the device and let it run. It worked. §6§lThe trait shifted permanently§r.
Higher-grade essences push the change further. Not sure the bees enjoy the process, but they do not seem worse off.
codex.apica.bees.essence_extractor.title: Essence Extractor
codex.apica.bees.essence_extractor.desc: Multiblock structure that distills bee essences.
Tried something. Placed a bee on the §6§lcentral pedestal§r and let honey drip from the §6§lreservoirs§r above.
Left it running overnight. By morning, small crystallized deposits had formed on the surrounding pedestals. §6§lEssences§r. They carry traces of the bee's own traits.
codex.apica.bees.meadow_bee.title: Meadow Bee
codex.apica.bees.meadow_bee.lore: First contact. I found them buzzing over wildflowers in an open field. Their honey glows faintly when held to the light. There is something more to these creatures than meets the eye.
codex.apica.bees.meadow_bee.desc: A peaceful bee found in flower-filled meadows, producing sweet honeycomb.
codex.apica.bees.forest_bee.title: Forest Bee
codex.apica.bees.forest_bee.lore: Spotted several specimens weaving between oak branches at dawn. The hum they produce resonates at an unusual frequency. Traces of pollen cling to the bark long after they depart.
codex.apica.bees.forest_bee.desc: Thrives among the trees, gathering pollen from woodland flowers.
codex.apica.bees.rocky_bee.title: Rocky Bee
codex.apica.bees.rocky_bee.lore: Found nesting in stone crevices. When agitated, their wings emit a faint mineral shimmer. My energy readings spike near geological formations.
codex.apica.bees.rocky_bee.desc: A hardy bee that feeds on crystals, leaving behind cobblestone in its wake.
codex.apica.bees.river_bee.title: River Bee
codex.apica.bees.river_bee.lore: Observed hovering just above the waterline, never getting wet. The mist around them bends as if repelled by some unseen force emanating from their wings.
codex.apica.bees.river_bee.desc: Lives near flowing water, its honey has a refreshing quality.
codex.apica.bees.dune_bee.title: Dune Bee
codex.apica.bees.dune_bee.lore: Remarkably aggressive when approached. Sandstorms seem to energize them rather than hinder flight. Their stingers crackle with static electricity.
codex.apica.bees.dune_bee.desc: An aggressive desert dweller that collects sand grains in its combs.
codex.apica.bees.nether_bee.title: Nether Bee
codex.apica.bees.nether_bee.lore: Captured a specimen through the portal. It did not burn my gloves. The energy signature here is completely different: hotter, wilder, barely contained.
codex.apica.bees.nether_bee.desc: Born in hellfire, this aggressive bee has adapted to extreme heat.
codex.apica.bees.end_bee.title: End Bee
codex.apica.bees.end_bee.lore: I cannot explain how it survives in the void. The energy it emits disrupts my instruments at close range. Observation must remain at a safe distance.
codex.apica.bees.end_bee.desc: A mysterious bee from the void, touched by ender energy.
codex.apica.bees.monsty_bee.title: Monsty Bee
codex.apica.bees.monsty_bee.lore: Troubling behavior. This specimen shows no fear of hostile creatures and seems drawn to them. The energy signature shifts dramatically in the presence of mobs, becoming more intense and erratic.
codex.apica.bees.monsty_bee.desc: A mischievous bee with a taste for monsters.
codex.apica.bees.docile_bee.title: Docile Bee
codex.apica.bees.docile_bee.lore: Remarkable temperament. This bee landed on my hand without any sign of hostility. Its energy output is gentle, almost warm. A soothing presence in the apiary.
codex.apica.bees.docile_bee.desc: The gentlest of bees, never harms a living soul.
codex.apica.bees.loam_bee.title: Loam Bee
codex.apica.bees.loam_bee.lore: Digs into topsoil with surprising efficiency. Under magnification, its legs show trace amounts of the same energy crystallized into the dirt it displaces.
codex.apica.bees.loam_bee.desc: Burrows in rich soil, producing dirt-infused combs.
codex.apica.bees.sediment_bee.title: Sediment Bee
codex.apica.bees.sediment_bee.lore: Observed sifting through riverbed gravel for hours. The energy field around it seems to separate particles by weight. A natural centrifuge of sorts.
codex.apica.bees.sediment_bee.desc: Sifts through riverbeds, collecting gravel particles.
codex.apica.bees.spark_bee.title: Spark Bee
codex.apica.bees.spark_bee.lore: Careful handling required. This one generates small discharges when stressed. The friction between its wing membranes and dry dust creates sparks. Or is it something else entirely?
codex.apica.bees.spark_bee.desc: Creates tiny sparks as it flies, leaving flint in its comb.
codex.apica.bees.cultural_bee.title: Cultural Bee
codex.apica.bees.cultural_bee.lore: Fascinating. This specimen watches and mimics patterns. I caught it following a farmer's routine through the fields. It seems drawn to cultivated land more than any wildflower patch.
codex.apica.bees.cultural_bee.desc: A civilized bee that learned from watching farmers.
codex.apica.bees.carbon_bee.title: Carbon Bee
codex.apica.bees.carbon_bee.lore: Only active after sunset. The energy it emits absorbs visible light, making it nearly invisible in darkness. Coal deposits seem to amplify its signal.
codex.apica.bees.carbon_bee.desc: Nocturnal bee that gathers coal fragments in darkness.
codex.apica.bees.cupric_bee.title: Cupric Bee
codex.apica.bees.cupric_bee.lore: The green patina on its wings is not oxidation. It is alive, growing. The specimen is drawn to copper veins as if magnetized by the metal itself.
codex.apica.bees.cupric_bee.desc: Attracted to oxidized metals, produces raw copper shards.
codex.apica.bees.festering_bee.title: Festering Bee
codex.apica.bees.festering_bee.lore: Unpleasant to handle. It feeds on organic decay and the energy it produces carries a foul odor. The combs I collected smelled of rot for days after extraction.
codex.apica.bees.festering_bee.desc: Feeds on decay, leaving rotten flesh in its comb.
codex.apica.bees.skeletal_bee.title: Skeletal Bee
codex.apica.bees.skeletal_bee.lore: Eerie. Its exoskeleton has a bone-white sheen and it makes no buzzing sound at all. The silence around it feels deliberate, almost predatory.
codex.apica.bees.skeletal_bee.desc: A ghastly bee that collects bones from fallen creatures.
codex.apica.bees.silken_bee.title: Silken Bee
codex.apica.bees.silken_bee.lore: Its comb is unlike any other, threaded with impossibly fine filaments. Under the lens, each thread pulses with microscopic energy channels.
codex.apica.bees.silken_bee.desc: Spins silk-like threads into its honeycomb.
codex.apica.bees.arachnid_bee.title: Arachnid Bee
codex.apica.bees.arachnid_bee.lore: Unsettling. Extra limb segments, unusual mandibles. I counted eight appendages. This is no ordinary mutation. The energy pattern is web-like, branching outward.
codex.apica.bees.arachnid_bee.desc: Eight-legged hybrid that produces spider eyes.
codex.apica.bees.tanner_bee.title: Tanner Bee
codex.apica.bees.tanner_bee.lore: Its saliva contains an enzyme I have never catalogued before. Leather scraps left near the hive were softened overnight. Remarkable biochemical potential.
codex.apica.bees.tanner_bee.desc: Its enzymes can process hide into leather fragments.
codex.apica.bees.plume_bee.title: Plume Bee
codex.apica.bees.plume_bee.lore: Incredibly quick. Sheds tiny feather-like scales in flight that float for minutes. The energy around it creates micro-updrafts, so it barely needs to flap.
codex.apica.bees.plume_bee.desc: A swift and light bee that sheds feathers as it flies.
codex.apica.bees.quercus_bee.title: Quercus Bee
codex.apica.bees.quercus_bee.lore: Nests exclusively in old-growth oak. It strips tiny fibers from the bark and weaves them into its comb. The resulting material is remarkably sturdy, like compressed wood.
codex.apica.bees.quercus_bee.desc: Nests in mighty oaks, producing oak log fragments.
codex.apica.bees.boreal_bee.title: Boreal Bee
codex.apica.bees.boreal_bee.lore: Functions in sub-zero conditions where ordinary bees would freeze. Its body temperature reads colder than ambient air. I have yet to determine how its wings avoid icing over.
codex.apica.bees.boreal_bee.desc: Thrives in cold forests, collecting spruce essence.
codex.apica.bees.tropical_bee.title: Tropical Bee
codex.apica.bees.tropical_bee.lore: Hyperactive in humid conditions. The energy it releases smells of rain and warm bark. Its comb carries a distinct tropical fragrance that lingers on the hands for hours.
codex.apica.bees.tropical_bee.desc: Dwells in humid jungles, gathering jungle wood.
codex.apica.bees.paperbark_bee.title: Paperbark Bee
codex.apica.bees.paperbark_bee.lore: Prefers pale-barked trees. Its comb has a paper-thin texture and the fibers appear to conduct energy like tiny wires running through the structure.
codex.apica.bees.paperbark_bee.desc: Loves birch trees, creating pale wood fragments.
codex.apica.bees.reed_bee.title: Reed Bee
codex.apica.bees.reed_bee.lore: Found nesting inside hollow sugar cane stalks. The honey is abnormally sweet. The sugar concentration exceeds anything that should be naturally possible.
codex.apica.bees.reed_bee.desc: Lives among sugar cane, sweetening its honey further.
codex.apica.bees.karot_bee.title: Karot Bee
codex.apica.bees.karot_bee.lore: This one is drawn to root vegetables with peculiar intensity. I found its comb stained deep orange, and the pollen baskets carried traces of carotene. A highly specialized forager.
codex.apica.bees.tuber_bee.title: Tuber Bee
codex.apica.bees.tuber_bee.lore: Tunnels underground following root systems with purpose. Its energy signature is strongest below the surface. Earth itself seems to amplify the signal.
codex.apica.bees.tuber_bee.desc: Burrows underground to tend potato crops.
codex.apica.bees.juicy_bee.title: Juicy Bee
codex.apica.bees.juicy_bee.lore: Produces an unusually sweet and viscous honey. The pollen it collects has a distinct melon scent, and its comb retains moisture far longer than any other species I have studied.
codex.apica.bees.juicy_bee.desc: Produces refreshing melon slices in its comb.
codex.apica.bees.gourd_bee.title: Gourd Bee
codex.apica.bees.gourd_bee.lore: Observed it methodically hollowing out a pumpkin to use as a secondary nest. Its energy pulses have a seasonal rhythm, strongest near autumn equinox.
codex.apica.bees.gourd_bee.desc: A festive bee that grows miniature pumpkins.
codex.apica.bees.agrarian_bee.title: Agrarian Bee
codex.apica.bees.agrarian_bee.lore: Works in tandem with crops like a tiny farmhand. It follows the same flight path between wheat stalks every cycle, never deviating. A creature of extraordinary routine and discipline.
codex.apica.bees.agrarian_bee.desc: The farmer's friend, cultivating wheat with care.
codex.apica.bees.prince_bee.title: Prince Bee
codex.apica.bees.prince_bee.lore: Noble bearing. Other bees defer to its presence, clearing paths as it flies. The energy signature is more refined, almost regal. A natural leader among its kind.
codex.apica.bees.prince_bee.desc: A noble bee of royal lineage, spawned naturally.
codex.apica.bees.flower_bee.title: Flower Bee
codex.apica.bees.flower_bee.lore: Visits every flower species indiscriminately. Its pollen baskets contain a blend that no single flower could produce. Cross-pollination elevated to an art form.
codex.apica.bees.flower_bee.desc: Pollinates all flowers, carrying precious flower pollen.
codex.apica.bees.mushroom_bee.title: Mushroom Bee
codex.apica.bees.mushroom_bee.lore: Thrives in complete darkness. The bioluminescent spots on its body match the glow of the spores it carries. A deep symbiotic relationship with fungi.
codex.apica.bees.mushroom_bee.desc: Thrives in darkness, spreading mushroom spores.
codex.apica.bees.tree_bee.title: Tree Bee
codex.apica.bees.tree_bee.lore: No preference for a single tree species. I watched it travel between oak, birch, and spruce within a single hour. A generalist of remarkable range.
codex.apica.bees.tree_bee.desc: Master of all forests, collecting tree pollen.
codex.apica.bees.crystal_bee.title: Crystal Bee
codex.apica.bees.crystal_bee.lore: Breakthrough observation. The crystallized honey it produces refracts energy in spectra I cannot identify. This may be the key to understanding the source.
codex.apica.bees.crystal_bee.desc: Crystallizes honey into magical crystal pollen.
codex.apica.bees.argil_bee.title: Argil Bee
codex.apica.bees.argil_bee.lore: Shapes clay with mechanical precision using mandible pressure alone. The finished balls are perfectly spherical. I measured a deviation of less than a millimeter.
codex.apica.bees.argil_bee.desc: Shapes clay balls with remarkable precision.
codex.apica.bees.umbra_bee.title: Umbra Bee
codex.apica.bees.umbra_bee.lore: Only appears beneath dense canopy where light barely reaches. Its dark wings seem to absorb even the faintest photons. The energy signature reads inverted.
codex.apica.bees.umbra_bee.desc: Dwells in shadowy groves of dark oak.
codex.apica.bees.savanna_bee.title: Savanna Bee
codex.apica.bees.savanna_bee.lore: Adapted to extreme drought. It extracts moisture from acacia bark and stores it as energy rather than water. An entirely novel metabolic pathway.
codex.apica.bees.savanna_bee.desc: Roams dry grasslands, collecting acacia wood.
codex.apica.bees.frost_bee.title: Frost Bee
codex.apica.bees.frost_bee.lore: Its comb is cold to the touch even in warm weather. The ice fragments embedded in the honeycomb do not melt under normal conditions. The energy it carries has a distinct chill.
codex.apica.bees.frost_bee.desc: A cold-loving bee that freezes water into ice fragments.
codex.apica.bees.volatile_bee.title: Volatile Bee
codex.apica.bees.volatile_bee.lore: DANGER. Keep away from open flames. The sulfurous compound in its comb is chemically identical to gunpowder. Its energy signature is unstable, erratic.
codex.apica.bees.volatile_bee.desc: Handle with care! Produces gunpowder fragments.
codex.apica.bees.ashen_bee.title: Ashen Bee
codex.apica.bees.ashen_bee.lore: Emerged from volcanic ash deposits. Its body temperature runs significantly higher than other specimens. The grey dust it leaves behind is warm to the touch.
codex.apica.bees.ashen_bee.desc: Born from volcanic ash, leaving andesite in its wake.
codex.apica.bees.igneous_bee.title: Igneous Bee
codex.apica.bees.igneous_bee.lore: Born from deep underground heat. The energy it carries smells of superheated stone. Its exoskeleton shows mineral inclusions that I have yet to identify.
codex.apica.bees.igneous_bee.desc: Forged in magma chambers, producing diorite.
codex.apica.bees.rose_stone_bee.title: Rose Stone Bee
codex.apica.bees.rose_stone_bee.lore: Attracted to pink quartz veins in exposed rock faces. The granite fragments in its comb contain trace amounts of crystallized energy, faintly glowing under certain light.
codex.apica.bees.rose_stone_bee.desc: Attracted to pink minerals, creating granite.
codex.apica.bees.infernalist_bee.title: Infernalist Bee
codex.apica.bees.infernalist_bee.lore: I should not have bred this one. The energy readings are overwhelming. My instruments overloaded twice. It regards me with what I can only describe as intelligence.
codex.apica.bees.infernalist_bee.desc: A demonic bee spawned in the depths of the Nether.
codex.apica.bees.swift_bee.title: Swift Bee
codex.apica.bees.swift_bee.lore: I cannot track its flight path with the naked eye. Frame-by-frame analysis shows it changes direction mid-wingbeat. The energy output is almost entirely kinetic.
codex.apica.bees.swift_bee.desc: Incredibly fast, a blur of golden wings.
codex.apica.bees.royal_bee.title: Royal Bee
codex.apica.bees.royal_bee.lore: Two prince bees were required. The result is magnificent, a bee that produces comb of incomparable quality. Other specimens instinctively gather to protect it.
codex.apica.bees.royal_bee.desc: Produces the coveted Royal Comb, fit for a queen.
codex.apica.bees.ferrous_bee.title: Ferrous Bee
codex.apica.bees.ferrous_bee.lore: Metal filings cling to its body in flight. My compass spins erratically when it passes. The energy field it generates is undeniably magnetic in nature.
codex.apica.bees.ferrous_bee.desc: Magnetized to iron, producing raw iron shards.
codex.apica.bees.flux_bee.title: Flux Bee
codex.apica.bees.flux_bee.lore: Its body carries a faint reddish glow that pulses in a steady rhythm. The redstone dust in its comb retains this pulsing quality even after extraction. An extraordinary specimen.
codex.apica.bees.flux_bee.desc: Channels energy through its body, dropping redstone dust.
codex.apica.bees.lazuli_bee.title: Lazuli Bee
codex.apica.bees.lazuli_bee.lore: Deep blue coloring unlike any pigment I have encountered. The lapis fragments in its comb contain energy patterns arranged in repeating geometric sequences.
codex.apica.bees.lazuli_bee.desc: Deep blue in color, producing lapis lazuli fragments.
codex.apica.bees.quartzose_bee.title: Quartzose Bee
codex.apica.bees.quartzose_bee.lore: Survives Nether conditions while producing crystalline structures. The quartz it generates is purer than any mined sample, perfectly ordered at the molecular level.
codex.apica.bees.quartzose_bee.desc: Thrives in Nether heat, creating quartz fragments.
codex.apica.bees.luminous_bee.title: Luminous Bee
codex.apica.bees.luminous_bee.lore: Its body emits a warm golden glow, brightest at the wing tips. The glowstone dust woven into its comb continues to shine faintly long after the bee has departed.
codex.apica.bees.luminous_bee.desc: Glows brightly in darkness, shedding glowstone dust.
codex.apica.bees.grim_bee.title: Grim Bee
codex.apica.bees.grim_bee.lore: Found in the basalt deltas among jagged spires. Its energy signature is somber, heavy, like standing in a graveyard. The blackstone it produces feels unnaturally cold.
codex.apica.bees.grim_bee.desc: A dark bee from the basalt deltas, producing blackstone.
codex.apica.bees.columnar_bee.title: Columnar Bee
codex.apica.bees.columnar_bee.lore: Constructs vertical structures from basalt with architectural precision. Each column segment locks perfectly into the next. Engineered by pure instinct.
codex.apica.bees.columnar_bee.desc: Builds towering structures from basalt columns.
codex.apica.bees.viscous_bee.title: Viscous Bee
codex.apica.bees.viscous_bee.lore: Produces a slime-like substance that defies normal viscosity. It bounces. The energy within provides unusual elastic properties to everything it touches.
codex.apica.bees.inky_bee.title: Inky Bee
codex.apica.bees.inky_bee.lore: Trails dark fluid in flight. The ink responds to nearby energy fields. I saw it form patterns on parchment without any directed motion, almost like writing.
codex.apica.bees.inky_bee.desc: Leaves trails of ink wherever it goes.
codex.apica.bees.fleece_bee.title: Fleece Bee
codex.apica.bees.fleece_bee.lore: Its pollen is soft, fibrous, closer to wool than any plant material. Under the lens, each fiber is hollow, filled with trace amounts of stored energy.
codex.apica.bees.fleece_bee.desc: Soft and fluffy, producing wool from pollen.
codex.apica.bees.adobe_bee.title: Adobe Bee
codex.apica.bees.adobe_bee.lore: Sun-bakes its own building material using internal heat. The terracotta it produces is harder than kiln-fired clay. Energy acts as an internal furnace during construction.
codex.apica.bees.adobe_bee.desc: Bakes clay under the desert sun into terracotta.
codex.apica.bees.geode_bee.title: Geode Bee
codex.apica.bees.geode_bee.lore: Grows amethyst structures inside its hive over several days. I cracked one open and found perfectly formed crystals, humming faintly with stored energy.
codex.apica.bees.geode_bee.desc: Grows beautiful amethyst shards in its hive.
codex.apica.bees.marsh_bee.title: Marsh Bee
codex.apica.bees.marsh_bee.lore: Navigates waterlogged terrain with ease. Its energy creates tiny air pockets around its body, keeping it completely dry even in the murkiest swamp conditions.
codex.apica.bees.marsh_bee.desc: Navigates murky swamps, gathering mangrove wood.
codex.apica.bees.frogish_bee.title: Frogish Bee
codex.apica.bees.frogish_bee.lore: Born from the union of fungal spores and swamp essence, this specimen has developed true bioluminescence. Its glow shifts between green, pink, and amber, mirroring the strange lights that frogs produce when fed certain glowing organisms.
codex.apica.bees.frogish_bee.desc: A bioluminescent bee whose comb yields rare froglights.
codex.apica.bees.hyphae_bee.title: Hyphae Bee
codex.apica.bees.hyphae_bee.lore: This specimen thrives in the crimson forests of the Nether, weaving through fungal networks with unsettling ease. Its body has adapted to the extreme heat, developing a deep burgundy exoskeleton.
codex.apica.bees.hyphae_bee.desc: Harvests crimson stems from Nether fungal forests.
codex.apica.bees.warped_bee.title: Warped Bee
codex.apica.bees.warped_bee.lore: A curious mutation born from quartz-dwelling ancestors and forest stock. Its cyan coloration and twisted flight patterns suggest dimensional instability.
codex.apica.bees.warped_bee.desc: Cultivates warped stems with an eerie cyan glow.
codex.apica.bees.shroomglow_bee.title: Shroomglow Bee
codex.apica.bees.shroomglow_bee.lore: The offspring of fungal and luminous bloodlines, this bee has internalized the light-producing chemistry of giant fungi. Its abdomen pulses with warm orange light in darkness.
codex.apica.bees.majestic_bee.title: Majestic Bee
codex.apica.bees.majestic_bee.lore: The culmination of the royal bloodline. When it enters the apiary, every other bee stops and orients toward it. The energy field is immense, almost palpable.
codex.apica.bees.majestic_bee.desc: The pride of any apiary, radiating regal beauty.
codex.apica.bees.steady_bee.title: Steady Bee
codex.apica.bees.steady_bee.lore: Displays a remarkably stable energy signature, unwavering under all conditions I have tested. Where other specimens show erratic readings under stress, this one remains perfectly constant.
codex.apica.bees.steady_bee.desc: Unwavering and reliable, immune to most threats.
codex.apica.bees.wind_bee.title: Wind Bee
codex.apica.bees.wind_bee.lore: Creates localized air currents strong enough to rustle papers across my lab. The pollen it carries swirls in perpetual motion, never settling on any surface.
codex.apica.bees.wind_bee.desc: Commands the winds, carrying Pollen of Wind.
codex.apica.bees.thunder_bee.title: Thunder Bee
codex.apica.bees.thunder_bee.lore: Its wings crackle with small electrical discharges during flight. The buzzing it produces resonates like distant rolling thunder. Storms seem to agitate rather than frighten this specimen.
codex.apica.bees.thunder_bee.desc: Crackles with electricity, bearing Pollen of Thunder.
codex.apica.bees.water_bee.title: Water Bee
codex.apica.bees.water_bee.lore: Perfectly at ease in rain and waterlogged environments. Its comb has a damp, oceanic quality, and the pollen it carries shimmers like light through shallow water.
codex.apica.bees.water_bee.desc: Master of aquatic realms, carrying Pollen of Water.
codex.apica.bees.fire_bee.title: Fire Bee
codex.apica.bees.fire_bee.lore: Its exoskeleton radiates a gentle warmth even at rest. The pollen it carries remains warm to the touch hours after collection. Handle the comb with gloves, as it retains heat.
codex.apica.bees.fire_bee.desc: Burns with inner flame, bearing Pollen of Fire.
codex.apica.bees.auric_bee.title: Auric Bee
codex.apica.bees.auric_bee.lore: Its wings catch light in a way that makes them appear solid gold. The raw gold in its comb is of exceptional purity. My last assay read ninety-nine percent.
codex.apica.bees.auric_bee.desc: Golden-winged treasure hunter, producing raw gold fragments.
codex.apica.bees.magmatic_bee.title: Magmatic Bee
codex.apica.bees.magmatic_bee.lore: Bathes in lava as others bathe in sunlight. I watched it submerge completely and emerge unharmed. The energy creates an absolute thermal barrier around its body.
codex.apica.bees.magmatic_bee.desc: Swims through lava, creating magma cream shards.
codex.apica.bees.spirit_bee.title: Spirit Bee
codex.apica.bees.spirit_bee.lore: Translucent in certain lighting conditions. At times it seems to flicker, as if not fully present in this plane. The soul sand residue on its legs smells of ancient ash.
codex.apica.bees.spirit_bee.desc: A ghostly bee that haunts soul sand valleys.
codex.apica.bees.crimson_bee.title: Crimson Bee
codex.apica.bees.crimson_bee.lore: Drawn instinctively to crimson fungi and nether wart. The spores clinging to its body have an unusual deep red hue. Its comb carries a pungent, earthy warmth unique among my specimens.
codex.apica.bees.crimson_bee.desc: Cultivates nether wart in crimson forests.
codex.apica.bees.mycelial_bee.title: Mycelial Bee
codex.apica.bees.mycelial_bee.lore: Its legs are perpetually coated in fine fungal spores. The comb it builds has a spongy, mycelial texture unlike any other species. The energy signature carries organic, earthy undertones.
codex.apica.bees.mycelial_bee.desc: Spreads fungal mycelium wherever it lands.
codex.apica.bees.humus_bee.title: Humus Bee
codex.apica.bees.humus_bee.lore: Nests at ground level, always in contact with soil. Its comb incorporates decomposed organic matter, producing a rich, dark material. The energy it emits carries the smell of deep forest earth.
codex.apica.bees.humus_bee.desc: Enriches soil into podzol with its activities.
codex.apica.bees.floral_bee.title: Floral Bee
codex.apica.bees.floral_bee.lore: A gardener of extraordinary dedication. Its pollen baskets carry a blend from dozens of flower species simultaneously. The resulting comb has a fragrance layered and complex.
codex.apica.bees.floral_bee.desc: A royal gardener that spreads flowers everywhere.
codex.apica.bees.treasure_bee.title: Treasure Bee
codex.apica.bees.treasure_bee.lore: Its hive glitters with embedded fragments of gold, gems, and rare minerals. How it locates these materials remains a mystery. The energy readings near its comb are unlike any other species.
codex.apica.bees.siphoning_bee.title: Siphoning Bee
codex.apica.bees.siphoning_bee.lore: The energy signature of this specimen mirrors that of End crystals. Whether it draws from the same source or merely resonates with it, I cannot determine. A deeply unsettling specimen.
codex.apica.bees.siphoning_bee.desc: Drains energy from the End dimension.
codex.apica.bees.zephyr_bee.title: Zephyr Bee
codex.apica.bees.zephyr_bee.lore: I blinked and lost it. My fastest recording device captured only a blur. Even the swift bee seems sluggish in comparison. I have never documented anything moving at this velocity.
codex.apica.bees.zephyr_bee.desc: The fastest bee ever recorded, a mere blur.
codex.apica.bees.prismatic_bee.title: Prismatic Bee
codex.apica.bees.prismatic_bee.lore: Light refracts through its wings into impossible spectra, colors I have no names for. The prismarine it produces vibrates at deep oceanic frequencies.
codex.apica.bees.crystalline_bee.title: Crystalline Bee
codex.apica.bees.crystalline_bee.lore: The prismarine crystals it creates are optically perfect. When I held one to the light, it projected constellations onto my wall that I did not recognize.
codex.apica.bees.crystalline_bee.desc: Creates prismarine crystals of perfect clarity.
codex.apica.bees.insomnia_bee.title: Insomnia Bee
codex.apica.bees.insomnia_bee.lore: Never sleeps. I monitored it for seventy-two hours straight and not once did it rest. The energy sustains it in a state of perpetual, tireless wakefulness.
codex.apica.bees.blazing_bee.title: Blazing Bee
codex.apica.bees.blazing_bee.lore: Its body temperature exceeds the melting point of lead. I can only handle it with reinforced gloves. The blaze rod fragments continue to glow days after extraction.
codex.apica.bees.blazing_bee.desc: Burns with infernal fury, producing blaze rod fragments.
codex.apica.bees.diamantine_bee.title: Diamantine Bee
codex.apica.bees.diamantine_bee.lore: The diamond dust it produces is not compressed carbon. It is something else entirely, something harder. My sharpest cutting tools cannot scratch it.
codex.apica.bees.diamantine_bee.desc: The ultimate gem collector, producing diamond dust.
codex.apica.bees.venerable_bee.title: Venerable Bee
codex.apica.bees.venerable_bee.lore: Moves with the deliberation of something ancient. It examined my equipment methodically, turning each instrument over with its legs. It was studying me back.
codex.apica.bees.venerable_bee.desc: Ancient and wise, creating precious emerald dust.
codex.apica.bees.volcanic_bee.title: Volcanic Bee
codex.apica.bees.volcanic_bee.lore: Born from eruption. The obsidian dust it sheds is glass-smooth and warm. I believe it liquefies stone with pure energy and lets it cool inside its comb.
codex.apica.bees.volcanic_bee.desc: Born in eruptions, producing obsidian dust.
codex.apica.bees.xenolith_bee.title: Xenolith Bee
codex.apica.bees.xenolith_bee.lore: Composed partly of end stone. Its body seems to exist in two places at once, a spatial anomaly that my instruments can barely register, let alone explain.
codex.apica.bees.xenolith_bee.desc: Stranger from beyond, leaving end stone behind.
codex.apica.bees.traveler_bee.title: Traveler Bee
codex.apica.bees.traveler_bee.lore: Disappeared from a sealed container and reappeared outside it. No hole, no gap, no explanation. The teleportation is real and leaves measurable ender residue.
codex.apica.bees.traveler_bee.desc: Teleports short distances, shedding ender pearl dust.
codex.apica.bees.sorrow_bee.title: Sorrow Bee
codex.apica.bees.sorrow_bee.lore: Its cry is the saddest sound I have ever documented. The tears it sheds crystallize instantly into perfectly clear shards. I feel a deep unease near this one.
codex.apica.bees.sorrow_bee.desc: Cries tears that crystallize into ghast tear shards.
codex.apica.bees.absorbent_bee.title: Absorbent Bee
codex.apica.bees.absorbent_bee.lore: Its exoskeleton has a porous, sponge-like texture unique among all specimens. The comb it produces can hold an extraordinary amount of moisture. Lightweight when dry, surprisingly heavy when saturated.
codex.apica.bees.absorbent_bee.desc: Soaks up water, creating sponge material.
codex.apica.bees.light_bee.title: Light Bee
codex.apica.bees.light_bee.lore: Pure radiance. It hurts to look at directly. The pollen it carries does not reflect light, it generates it. A primary source of luminous energy unlike anything known.
codex.apica.bees.light_bee.desc: Radiates pure light, carrying Pollen of Light.
codex.apica.bees.dark_bee.title: Dark Bee
codex.apica.bees.dark_bee.lore: Its wings are so dark they seem to swallow the light around them. Photographs of this specimen always appear underexposed regardless of lighting. The pollen it carries is pitch-black.
codex.apica.bees.dark_bee.desc: Absorbs all light, bearing Pollen of Darkness.
codex.apica.bees.imperial_bee.title: Imperial Bee
codex.apica.bees.imperial_bee.lore: The most imposing specimen I have ever catalogued. Its energy signature dwarfs every other bee in the apiary. When I hold its comb, I feel a weight that has nothing to do with mass.
codex.apica.bees.imperial_bee.desc: Rules over all other bees with absolute authority.
codex.apica.bees.demonic_bee.title: Demonic Bee
codex.apica.bees.demonic_bee.lore: Something went wrong. The energy has turned malevolent, hostile. It broke three containment fields before I restrained it. I hear faint whispers near its hive at night.
codex.apica.bees.demonic_bee.desc: A terrifying bee corrupted by dark forces.
codex.apica.bees.paladin_bee.title: Paladin Bee
codex.apica.bees.paladin_bee.lore: A counterbalance to the demonic. Its energy signature is the exact inverse of the demonic bee's readings. In its presence, the instruments that malfunction near demonic specimens stabilize entirely.
codex.apica.bees.paladin_bee.desc: A holy warrior bee that smites hostile creatures.
codex.apica.bees.chorus_bee.title: Chorus Bee
codex.apica.bees.chorus_bee.lore: Teleports erratically and without warning. Mid-sentence in my notes, it appeared on my pen. The chorus fragments in its comb shimmer with a faint purple displacement haze.
codex.apica.bees.ancient_bee.title: Ancient Bee
codex.apica.bees.ancient_bee.lore: Dating analysis returned impossible results. This specimen appears older than the bedrock itself. The netherite traces in its comb predate any known geological record.
codex.apica.bees.ancient_bee.desc: Primordial bee that produces netherite scrap dust.
codex.apica.bees.void_bee.title: Void Bee
codex.apica.bees.void_bee.lore: Exists at the threshold between light and darkness. Neither one nor the other. The energy it carries is the absence of both, a null frequency I cannot measure.
codex.apica.bees.void_bee.desc: Exists between light and dark, carrying Void Pollen.
codex.apica.bees.draconic_bee.title: Draconic Bee
codex.apica.bees.draconic_bee.lore: The energy signature matches readings I took near the Ender Dragon itself. Impossible, yet undeniable. The dragon breath it produces is identical to the beast's own.
codex.apica.bees.draconic_bee.desc: Infused with dragon essence, producing dragon breath dust.
codex.apica.bees.stellar_bee.title: Stellar Bee
codex.apica.bees.stellar_bee.lore: The nether star dust in its comb defies analysis. Every instrument I use returns different readings each time. I believe this bee channels forces that operate beyond the scope of my understanding.
codex.apica.bees.stellar_bee.desc: Born from a dying star, shedding nether star dust.
gui.apica.network.queue_full: Request queue full — try again later
codex.apica.apica.apica.title: Apica
codex.apica.apica.apica.desc: First observations on the bees of this world.
codex.apica.apica.apica.lore.p1: Noticed something odd with the local bees. A flower wilted right after one landed on it. Checked the soil, checked the light. Nothing explains it. Probably coincidence.
codex.apica.apica.apica.lore.p2: Happened again. Different bee, different flower. The hive nearby hums louder than it should. Touched the bark next to it and it felt warm. Each thing on its own means nothing. But together, it nags at me. Worth looking into.
codex.apica.apica.1st_bee.title: New bee
codex.apica.apica.1st_bee.desc: Found a scoop design in old notes.
codex.apica.apica.1st_bee.lore: Can't learn much just watching. I need a specimen. A §6§lScoop§r should do the job. From what I can tell, there are about five different species around here. Might as well start with whichever one I find first.
codex.apica.apica.1st_bee.lore2: I should also §6§lbreak the hive§r to collect some honey.
codex.apica.apica.hive.title: Hive
codex.apica.apica.hive.desc: A controlled habitat for captured bees.
codex.apica.apica.hive.lore.p1: Got my first specimen. Up close, the energy readings are unmistakable. But it will not survive long in a jar. I need a proper habitat, something that mimics a natural nest while allowing observation. Found old schematics for a §6§lHive§r that should work.
codex.apica.apica.hive.lore.p2: The bee settled in immediately. It leaves at dawn, forages nearby flowers, and returns with comb material. The comb has a faint shimmer under direct light. Each species seems to produce a different type. I need to process these somehow.
codex.apica.apica.manual_centrifuge.lore: The combs contain two distinct components: a liquid fraction and solid particulate in the wax. A §6§lManual Centrifuge§r paired with a §6§lCrank§r should separate them.
Confirmed. The fragments vary with comb type. Some contain mineral traces, others organic material. The liquid honey behaves strangely. Left in the tank, it hums.
codex.apica.apica.crystallyzer.title: Crystallizer
codex.apica.apica.crystallyzer.lore.p1: The liquid honey behaves unlike any natural substance I have catalogued. Left undisturbed in a sealed vessel, it begins to form crystalline lattice structures on its own. Slow, but repeatable across every sample.
codex.apica.apica.crystallyzer.lore.p2: Built a §6§lCrystallizer§r to accelerate the process. The resulting §6§lHoney Crystals§r are solid, warm to the touch, and retain the same faint energy I keep measuring in the bees themselves. This is not ordinary honey. Something is stored in it.
codex.apica.apica.altar.title: Altar
codex.apica.apica.altar.lore.p1: The crystals react to proximity. Placed three on a workbench and they shifted, orienting toward the center. No magnets, no static charge. Something else entirely.
codex.apica.apica.altar.lore.p2: Designed a structure around this principle. An §6§lAltar Heart§r surrounded by §6§lReservoirs§r and §6§lPedestals§r. When activated, the crystals resonate and the input transforms. The results are consistent.
codex.apica.apica.hive_multibloc.title: Apiary
codex.apica.apica.hive_multibloc.lore.p1: One hive is not enough. I need more specimens running simultaneously, more data points, more comb variety. The current setup cannot keep pace with the questions I have.
codex.apica.apica.hive_multibloc.lore.p2: Assembled an §6§lApiary§r. Five bee slots instead of two. The increased density has an unexpected benefit: the bees coordinate. Production is noticeably higher. Real research begins now.
codex.apica.artifacts.building_staff.des: Condensed honey holds the shape of matter.
codex.apica.artifacts.building_staff.lor: I am starting to see a pattern in how honey reacts to its surroundings. Compressed a sample in a chamber next to a stone block. The honey held an afterimage of it. Clear and stable for a few seconds before it collapsed.
I have managed to almost fix the effect. The imprint holds, kind of. Now it is somewhat usable. If I point it at a block, it extends outward.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.title: Honey Lamp
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.desc: Crystals feed on honey, produce light.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.lore.p1: Submerged a rune in liquid honey to see what would happen. The reaction was immediate, a bright glow filled the room. It stayed stable, drawing from the honey slowly.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.lore.p2: Built a simple frame to hold it together. The lamp works well, the light holds as long as there is §6§lhoney§r to feed it.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.lore.p3: Tried §6§lRoyal Jelly§r. The light reaches further.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_lamp.lore.p4: With §6§lNectar§r, one lamp lit the full workshop.
codex.apica.artifacts.pollen_pot.title: Pollen Pot
codex.apica.artifacts.pollen_pot.desc: Stores a single type of pollen.
codex.apica.artifacts.pollen_pot.lore.p1: Pollen kept scattering across the workbench. Different types mixing together, ruining samples. Shaped a small clay pot to hold §6§lone pollen type§r at a time, up to sixteen doses.
codex.apica.artifacts.pollen_pot.lore.p2: Right-click to deposit, punch to retrieve. Keeps things organized. The incubator reads from it directly, which saves a step.
codex.apica.artifacts.api.title: Api
codex.apica.artifacts.api.desc: A honeycomb that seems to have come alive.
codex.apica.artifacts.api.lore.p1: One of the altar crafts went wrong. Or right. Hard to tell. The honeycomb at the center did not transform into what was expected. Instead it started... moving. Wiggling, almost.
codex.apica.artifacts.api.lore.p1b: Left it on the workbench. I think it is alive, but does it have a consciousness? Or is its head empty? Either way, it seems content just... being. Funny little creature.
codex.apica.artifacts.api.lore.p2: Tried feeding it a §6§lHoney Bottle§r. It swelled up happily. The growth is not instant, it takes a while to settle into its new size. Once it does, it pushes against anything nearby. Soft blocks crumble. But if the stubborn thing pushes into something harder, like iron, it pops. All that is left is a single honeycomb. Poor thing did not know its limits.
codex.apica.artifacts.api.lore.p3: Gave it a §6§lcomb§r instead. It shrank, looking almost offended. Good to know there is a way to keep it in check. A living honeycomb. What a world.
codex.apica.artifacts.sniper.title: Sniper
codex.apica.artifacts.chopper_axe.title: Chopper Axe
codex.apica.artifacts.bras_gun.title: Bras Gun
codex.apica.artifacts.bee_backpack.title: Companion: Backpack
codex.apica.artifacts.bee_backpack.desc: A companion bee carrying a portable chest.
Trained a worker bee to carry a small chest. The bee follows faithfully, hovering at shoulder height. Right-click the bee to access the storage anywhere.
codex.apica.artifacts.uncrafting_table.d: A table that reverses crafting recipes using Nectar.
codex.apica.artifacts.magnet.title: Companion: Magnet
codex.apica.artifacts.magnet.desc: A companion bee that picks up nearby items.
codex.apica.artifacts.magnet.lore.p1: Attuned a worker bee to detect dropped items. It flies out, picks them up, and brings them back. If your inventory is full, it drops the item at your feet.
codex.apica.artifacts.wave_mixer.title: Wave Mixer
codex.apica.artifacts.wave_mixer.desc: In-game music sequencer using Note Block instruments.
codex.apica.artifacts.wave_mixer.lore.p1: Salvaged a few circuits from note blocks. I need to see if bees are receptive to music, or if honey can have a positive response to it.
codex.apica.artifacts.wave_mixer.lore.p2: I started to see something. The behaviour of certain bees started to change. Maybe if I increase the complexity by adding some instruments I will see something.
codex.apica.artifacts.wave_mixer.lore.p3: The observation lead to a dead end. Honey is not receptive and bees are just liking the music but nothing particular to notice. At least I know what to do now if I want to change career.
codex.apica.artifacts.dubstep_gun.title: Dubstep Gun
codex.apica.artifacts.launchpad.title: Launchpad
codex.apica.artifacts.launchpad.desc: Pressurized plate. Propels entities on contact.
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_bread.title: Honey Bread
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_bread.lore.p: Dropped a loaf into the infuser by accident. The §6§lhoney§r soaked through and the §6§lbread§r came out denser, richer. Fills the stomach more than plain bread ever did.
codex.apica.artifacts.royal_bread.title: Royal Bread
codex.apica.artifacts.royal_bread.lore.p: Tried the same with §6§lRoyal Jelly§r. The texture is way different, I had to change the recipe and the form.
codex.apica.artifacts.nectar_bread.title: Nectar Bread
Soaked the bread in §6§lNectar§r. The crust shimmers and the smell is out of this world.
codex.apica.artifacts.anti_breeding_crys: A crystal that prevents bees from breeding.

Some hives were getting overcrowded. Found that placing this crystal on top of a hive stops the bees from breeding entirely. They still work, still produce, but no new larvae. Useful when you need to keep a colony stable without it spiraling out of control.
codex.apica.artifacts.chopper_hive.title: Chopper Hive
codex.apica.artifacts.chopper_hive.desc: A cube that fells entire trees.
While working on a control system, the swarm turned on itself. I think I can focus this behavior with runes.

The runes only amplified their aggressiveness. Destroying everything in their path. They ravaged the workshop, I barely made it out. After reversing the inscription, they started focusing only on wood.
codex.apica.artifacts.leaf_blower.title: Leaf Blower
codex.apica.artifacts.leaf_blower.desc: Compressed wind in a honeyed shell.
codex.apica.artifacts.leaf_blower.lore.p: Trapped air inside a sealed honey crystal chamber. The pressure built until the casing cracked. Instead of fixing it, I added a trigger. The burst tore every leaf off the oak outside the workshop.

The longer I hold, the stronger the blast. A quick tap clears a small patch. A full charge and the orb punches through canopy, sending out shockwaves that strip everything green in a wide radius. Flowers, ferns, vines. All of it, gone.
You will never admit that you need one. But you are happy when it exists.
P.S.: Is this too much for just a leaf blower?
codex.apica.artifacts.railgun.title: Railgun
codex.apica.artifacts.honey_artifact_cor: Stabilized honey energy. Base for artifacts.
Embedded a honey crystal into a frame of honeyed iron. The crystal pulses steadily, holding its charge. This seems stable enough to build upon.
codex.apica.artifacts.royal_artifact_cor: Replaced the honey crystal with a royal one. The energy output increased noticeably. The frame needed royal gold to handle the load without degrading.
codex.apica.artifacts.nectar_artifact_co: A nectar crystal at the center, surrounded by nectar-infused diamond. The energy is intense, almost uncomfortable to hold. Artifacts built from this will be something else entirely.
codex.apica.artifacts.accessories.title: Companion: Bee
codex.apica.artifacts.accessories.desc: A decorative companion bee that follows you.
codex.apica.artifacts.accessories.lore.p: The simplest form of bee companionship. A trained worker bee follows its keeper at shoulder height. No utility, no storage. Just a faithful presence.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_barrel.lor: Ran out of chest space again. Half of them contain the same material. Wasteful. Designed a Storage Barrel that accepts §6§lone item type§r only, but in far greater quantity.
The front face displays what is stored and how much. Punch to take, place to give. Simple enough.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_barrel_mk2: The first barrel filled up faster than expected. Need more depth. Lined the interior with honeyed iron and restructured the framing. The Storage Barrel MK2 holds roughly twice as much. Should buy me time.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_barrel_mk3: It was not enough. Production keeps scaling. Tested royal gold as internal framing. The material compresses stored goods without degrading them. The Storage Barrel MK3 replaces what used to take an entire wall of chests.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_barrel_mk4: Final iteration. Nectar diamond lining creates some kind of spatial compression I do not fully understand yet. The Storage Barrel MK4 holds more than I can produce in a reasonable timeframe. Storage is no longer the bottleneck.
codex.apica.logistics.void_upgrade.title: Void Upgrade
codex.apica.logistics.void_upgrade.lore: Full barrels clog the pipes. The whole chain stalls. Needed a relief valve. The Void Upgrade, applied directly to a barrel, §6§ldestroys any overflow§r silently. An indicator appears on the front face. Wasteful, but preferable to a jammed system.
codex.apica.logistics.trash_can.title: Item Trash Can
codex.apica.logistics.trash_can.lore.p1: Centrifuge byproducts keep piling up. No use for most of them and they clog the output. Built an Item Trash Can. Drop anything in and it is §6§ldestroyed§r.
codex.apica.logistics.trash_can.lore.p2: The last item stays visible as a reminder of what went in. Hooks into §6§lpipes§r and hoppers for continuous disposal.
codex.apica.logistics.liquid_trash_can.l: Excess honey and water overflow the tanks when I am not watching. The Liquid Trash Can §6§lvoids any fluid§r fed into it.
A bucket placed inside is emptied and returned. Connects to liquid §6§lpipes§r for automated drainage.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_hive.title: Storage Hive
codex.apica.logistics.storage_hive.lore: The network kept stalling. Not enough bees for the workload. Needed a way to house more of them near the controller. Took a few tries. The result fits into the §6§lcorner§r of the structure and the bees accepted it, so it works. The design came out... strange. Some kind of compromise between what I wanted and what the bees would tolerate. More §6§lsimultaneous tasks§r, though. That is what matters.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_hive_mk2.l: One hive per corner was not enough anymore. Reinforced the internals to fit a larger colony. The Storage Hive MK2 supports noticeably more bees. The network keeps up again, for now.
codex.apica.logistics.storage_hive_mk3.l: Pushed it as far as the design allows. Royal gold framing sustains a much denser colony. The Storage Hive MK3 handles everything I can throw at the network. If throughput becomes an issue again, the problem is elsewhere.
Did it just look at me?
